**Handler Struct**\n```go\ntype ExampleHandler struct {\n    mu      sync.RWMutex          // Thread-safe access\n    db      *sql.DB               // SQLite connection (if needed)\n    manager *SomeManager          // Business logic\n}\n\n// Getter methods with read locks\nfunc (h *ExampleHandler) Get() {...}\n\n// Registration method\nfunc (h *ExampleHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n    r.Get(\"/api/example\", h.handleGet)\n    r.Post(\"/api/example\", h.handleCreate)\n    r.Put(\"/api/example/{id}\", h.handleUpdate)\n    r.Delete(\"/api/example/{id}\", h.handleDelete)\n}\n```\n\n#### **Request Handling Pattern**\n```go\n// Handler reads JSON from request body\nfunc (h *ExampleHandler) handleCreate(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n    var req CreateRequest\n    \n    // Decode JSON from request body\n    if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n        writeJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \n            \"invalid request body: \"+err.Error())\n        return\n    }\n    \n    // Validate\n    if req.Name == \"\" {\n        writeJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"name required\")\n        return\n    }\n    \n    // Process with business logic\n    result, err := h.manager.Create(&req)\n    if err != nil {\n        writeJSONError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, \n            \"failed to create: \"+err.Error())\n        return\n    }\n    \n    // Write JSON response\n    writeJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, result)\n}\n```\n\n#### **Response Writing Utilities** (`/internal/api/utils.go`)\n```go\n// Write successful JSON response\nfunc writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v interface{})\n\n// Write error JSON response\nfunc writeJSONError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, message string)\n\n// Write JSON without status (assumes already set)\nfunc writeJSONData(w http.ResponseWriter, v interface{})\n```\n\n#### **SQLite Interaction Pattern**\n```go\n// Typically a handler manages its own DB or uses shared connection\ndb, _ := sql.Open(\"sqlite\", \"path/to/db\")\ndefer db.Close()\n\n// Query example\nrows, err := db.Query(\"SELECT id, name FROM entities WHERE ...\")\ndefer rows.Close()\n\nfor rows.Next() {\n    var id string\n    var name string\n    if err := rows.Scan(&id, &name); err != nil {...}\n    // **Handler Struct**\n```go\ntype ExampleHandler struct {\n    mu      sync.RWMutex          // Thread-safe access\n    db      *sql.DB               // SQLite connection (if needed)\n    manager *SomeManager          // Business logic\n}\n\n// Getter methods with read locks\nfunc (h *ExampleHandler) Get() {...}\n\n// Registration method\nfunc (h *ExampleHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n    r.Get(\"/api/example\", h.handleGet)\n    r.Post(\"/api/example\", h.handleCreate)\n    r.Put(\"/api/example/{id}\", h.handleUpdate)\n    r.Delete(\"/api/example/{id}\", h.handleDelete)\n}\n```\n\n#### **Request Handling Pattern**\n```go\n// Handler reads JSON from request body\nfunc (h *ExampleHandler) handleCreate(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n    var req CreateRequest\n    \n    // Decode JSON from request body\n    if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n        writeJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \n            \"invalid request body: \"+err.Error())\n        return\n    }\n    \n    // Validate\n    if req.Name == \"\" {\n        writeJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"name required\")\n        return\n    }\n    \n    // Process with business logic\n    result, err := h.manager.Create(&req)\n    if err != nil {\n        writeJSONError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, \n            \"failed to create: \"+err.Error())\n        return\n    }\n    \n    // Write JSON response\n    writeJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, result)\n}\n```\n\n#### **Response Writing Utilities** (`/internal/api/utils.go`)\n```go\n// Write successful JSON response\nfunc writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v interface{})\n\n// Write error JSON response\nfunc writeJSONError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, message string)\n\n// Write JSON without status (assumes already set)\nfunc writeJSONData(w http.ResponseWriter, v interface{})\n```\n\n#### **SQLite Interaction Pattern**\n```go\n// Typically a handler manages its own DB or uses shared connection\ndb, _ := sql.Open(\"sqlite\", \"path/to/db\")\ndefer db.Close()\n\n// Query example\nrows, err := db.Query(\"SELECT id, name FROM entities WHERE ...\")\ndefer rows.Close()\n\nfor rows.Next() {\n    var id string\n    var name string\n    if err := rows.Scan(&id, &name); err != nil {...}\n    // Process row\n}\n\n// Insert/Update example\nresult, err := db.Exec(\"INSERT INTO entities (id, name) VALUES (?, ?)\", id, name)\nlastID, err := result.LastInsertId()\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. **Router Versioning**: Via `schema_migrations` table with version tracking\n- **Migrations**: 15 migration functions in `migrations.go`\n\n#### **JSON Patterns**\n- Request/response bodies use `encoding/json`\n- Complex configs stored as JSON text in DB (e.g., `condition_params_json`)\n- Error responses: `{\"error\": \"message\"}`\n\n#### **Concurrency Safety**\n- All handlers use **201 Created** - Successful POST resource creation\n- **204 No Content** - Successful DELETE\n- **400 Bad Request** - Validation/decode errors\n- **404 Not Found** - Resource not found\n- **500 Internal Server Error** - Database/processing errors\n\n---\n\n### 8. **Important Files Summary**\n\n| File | Lines | Purpose |\n|------|-------|---------|\n| `/cmd/mothership/main.go` | 4,799 | Full /api/settings requests.\n288\t// It validates known settings and applies partial updates with merge semantics.\n289\tfunc (s *SettingsHandler) handleUpdateSettings(w http.ResponseWriter, r {\n298\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n299\t\t\treturn\n300\t\t}\n301\t\n302\t\t// Track edits for troubleshooting hints\n303\t\tvar hintPending settings\n335\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, settings)\n336\t}\n337\t\n338\t// validateSettings validates the provided settings values.\n339\t// Returns an error if any setting value is outside its Package api provides REST API handlers for Spaxel zones and portals.\npackage api\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5\"\n\t\"github.com/spaxel/mothership/internal/dashboard\"\n\t\"github.com/spaxel/mothership/internal/zones\"\n)\n\n// BlobIdentityProvider resolves blob IDs to person labels.\ntype BlobIdentityProvider interface {\n\t// PersonLabelForBlob returns the BLE-identified person label for a blob,\n\t// or an empty string if the blob is unidentified.\n\tPersonLabelForBlob(blobID int) string\n}\n\n// ZonesHandler manages zones and portals via the zones.Manager.\n// Changes to zones and portals are immediately broadcast to dashboard clients\n// via the ZoneChangeBroadcaster, and also reflected in the next delta tick.\ntype ZonesHandler struct {\n\tmu      sync.RWMutex\n\tmgr     *zones.Manager\n\tbc      dashboard.ZoneChangeBroadcaster\n\tident   BlobIdentityProvider\n}\n\n// zoneWithOcc extends a zone with current occupancy and people list for API responses.\ntype zoneWithOcc struct {\n\tID        string    `json:\"id\"`\n\tName      string    `json:\"name\"`\n\tColor     string    `json:\"color,omitempty\"`\n\tMinX      float64   `json:\"x\"`\n\tMinY      float64   `json:\"y\"`\n\tMinZ      float64   `json:\"z\"`\n\tMaxX      float64   `json:\"max_x\"`\n\tMaxY      float64   `json:\"max_y\"`\n\tMaxZ      float64   `json:\"max_z\"`\n\tWidth     float64   `json:\"w\"`\n\tDepth     float64   `json:\"d\"`\n\tHeight    float64   `json:\"h\"`\n\tEnabled   bool      `json:\"enabled\"`\n\tZoneType  string    `json:\"zone_type\"`\n\tOccupancy int       `json:\"occupancy\"`\n\tPeople    []string  `json:\"people\"`\n\tCreatedAt time.Time `json:\"created_at\"`\n}\n\n// portalWithZones extends a portal with resolved zone names for API responses.\ntype portalWithZones struct {\n\tID        string    `json:\"id\"`\n\tName      string    `json:\"name\"`\n\tZoneA     string    `json:\"zone_a\"`\n\tZoneB     string    `json:\"zone_b\"`\n\tP1X       float64   `json:\"p1_x\"`\n\tP1Y       float64   `json:\"p1_y\"`\n\tP1Z       float64   `json:\"p1_z\"`\n\tP2X       float64   `json:\"p2_x\"`\n\tP2Y       float64   `json:\"p2_y\"`\n\tP2Z       float64   `json:\"p2_z\"`\n\tP3X       float64   `json:\"p3_x\"`\n\tP3Y       float64   `json:\"p3_y\"`\n\tP3Z       float64   `json:\"p3_z\"`\n\tNX        float64   `json:\"n_x\"`\n\tNY        float64   `json:\"n_y\"`\n\tNZ        float64   `json:\"n_z\"`\n\tWidth     float64   `json:\"width\"`\n\tHeight    float64   `json:\"height\"`\n\tEnabled   bool      `json:\"enabled\"`\n\tCreatedAt time.Time `json:\"created_at\"`\n}\n\n// crossingResponse is a single crossing event as returned by the API.\ntype crossingResponse struct {\n\tID        int64     `json:\"id\"`\n\tPortalID  string    `json:\"portal_id\"`\n\tBlobID    int       `json:\"blob_id\"`\n\tDirection string    `json:\"direction\"`\n\tFromZone  string    `json:\"from_zone\"`\n\tToZone    string    `json:\"to_zone\"`\n\tTimestamp time.Time `json:\"timestamp\"`\n\tPerson    string    `json:\"person,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// NewZonesHandler creates a new zones handler backed by a zones.Manager.\nfunc NewZonesHandler(mgr *zones.Manager) *ZonesHandler {\n\treturn &ZonesHandler{mgr: mgr}\n}\n\n// SetZoneChangeBroadcaster sets the broadcaster for immediate WebSocket\n// notifications when zones or portals are modified.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) SetZoneChangeBroadcaster(bc dashboard.ZoneChangeBroadcaster) {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n\th.bc = bc\n}\n\n// SetBlobIdentityProvider sets the provider that resolves blob IDs to person labels.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) SetBlobIdentityProvider(p BlobIdentityProvider) {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n\th.ident = p\n}\n\n// notifyZoneChange broadcasts a zone change event if a broadcaster is set.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) notifyZoneChange(action string, z *zones.Zone) {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tbc := h.bc\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\tif bc == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tocc := h.mgr.GetZoneOccupancy(z.ID)\n\tcount := 0\n\tif occ != nil {\n\t\tcount = occ.Count\n\t}\n\tbc.BroadcastZoneChange(action, dashboard.ZoneSnapshot{\n\t\tID:    z.ID,\n\t\tName:  z.Name,\n\t\tCount: count,\n\t\tMinX:  z.MinX,\n\t\tMinY:  z.MinY,\n\t\tMinZ:  z.MinZ,\n\t\tSizeX: z.MaxX - z.MinX,\n\t\tSizeY: z.MaxY - z.MinY,\n\t\tSizeZ: z.MaxZ - z.MinZ,\n\t})\n}\n\n// notifyPortalChange broadcasts a portal change event if a broadcaster is set.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) notifyPortalChange(action string, p *zones.Portal) {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tbc := h.bc\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\tif bc == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tbc.BroadcastPortalChange(action, dashboard.PortalSnapshot{\n\t\tID:      p.ID,\n\t\tName:    p.Name,\n\t\tZoneA:   p.ZoneAID,\n\t\tZoneB:   p.ZoneBID,\n\t\tP1X:     p.P1X,\n\t\tP1Y:     p.P1Y,\n\t\tP1Z:     p.P1Z,\n\t\tP2X:     p.P2X,\n\t\tP2Y:     p.P2Y,\n\t\tP2Z:     p.P2Z,\n\t\tP3X:     p.P3X,\n\t\tP3Y:     p.P3Y,\n\t\tP3Z:     p.P3Z,\n\t\tNX:      p.NX,\n\t\tNY:      p.NY,\n\t\tNZ:      p.NZ,\n\t\tWidth:   p.Width,\n\t\tHeight:  p.Height,\n\t\tEnabled: p.Enabled,\n\t})\n}\n\n// Close closes the underlying manager.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) Close() error {\n\treturn h.mgr.Close()\n}\n\n// RegisterRoutes registers zones and portals endpoints.\n//\n// Zones:\n//\n//\tGET /api/zones\n//\n//\t@Summary\t\tList all Normal vector is auto-computed from the three defining points.\n//\t@Tags\t\t\tportals\n//\t@Accept\t\t\tjson\n//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n//\t@Param\t\t\tportal\tbody\t\tzones.Portal\ttrue\t\"Portal definition\"\n//\t@Success\t\t201\t{object}\tzones.Portal\t\"Created portal\"\n//\t@Failure\t\t400\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Invalid request body\"\n//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/portals [post]\n//\n//\tPUT /api/portals/{id}\n//\n//\t@Summary\t\tUpdate a portal\n//\t@Description\tUpdates an existing portal's properties. deleted\"\n//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/portals/{id} [delete]\n//\n//\tGET /api/portals/{id}/crossings\n//\n//\t@Summary\t\tPortal crossing log\n//\t@Description\tReturns recent directional crossings for a [get]\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n\t// Zones\n\tr.Get(\"/api/zones\", h.listZones)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/zones\", h.createZone)\n\tr.Put(\"/api/zones/{id}\", h.updateZone)\n\tr.Delete(\"/api/zones/{id}\", h.deleteZone)\n\tr.Get(\"/api/zones/{id}/history\", h.getZoneHistory)\n\n\t// Portals\n\tr.Get(\"/api/portals\", h.listPortals)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/portals\", h.createPortal)\n\tr.Put(\"/api/portals/{id}\", h.updatePortal)\n\tr.Delete(\"/api/portals/{id}\", h.deletePortal)\n\tr.Get(\"/api/portals/{id}/crossings\", h.getPortalCrossings)\n}\n\n// toZoneResponse converts a zones.Zone to the API response format with occupancy.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) toZoneResponse(z *zones.Zone) zoneWithOcc {\n\tocc := h.mgr.GetZoneOccupancy(z.ID)\n\tcount := 0\n\tvar people []string\n\tif occ != nil {\n\t\tcount = occ.Count\n\t\tpeople = h.resolvePeople(occ.BlobIDs)\n\t}\n\tif people == nil {\n\t\tpeople = []string{}\n\t}\n\treturn zoneWithOcc{\n\t\tID:        z.ID,\n\t\tName:      z.Name,\n\t\tColor:     z.Color,\n\t\tMinX:      z.MinX,\n\t\tMinY:      z.MinY,\n\t\tMinZ:      z.MinZ,\n\t\tMaxX:      z.MaxX,\n\t\tMaxY:      z.MaxY,\n\t\tMaxZ:      z.MaxZ,\n\t\tWidth:     z.MaxX - z.MinX,\n\t\tDepth:     z.MaxY - z.MinY,\n\t\tHeight:    z.MaxZ - z.MinZ,\n\t\tEnabled:   z.Enabled,\n\t\tZoneType:  string(z.ZoneType),\n\t\tOccupancy: count,\n\t\tPeople:    people,\n\t\tCreatedAt: z.CreatedAt,\n\t}\n}\n\n// resolvePeople maps blob IDs to deduplicated person labels.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) resolvePeople(blobIDs []int) []string {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tident := h.ident\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif ident == nil || len(blobIDs) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tseen := make(map[string]bool)\n\tvar people []string\n\tfor _, id := range blobIDs {\n\t\tlabel := ident.PersonLabelForBlob(id)\n\t\tif label != \"\" && !seen[label] {\n\t\t\tseen[label] = true\n\t\t\tpeople = append(people, label)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn people\n}\n\n// toPortalResponse converts a zones.Portal to the API response format.\nfunc toPortalResponse(p *zones.Portal) portalWithZones {\n\treturn portalWithZones{\n\t\tID:        p.ID,\n\t\tName:      p.Name,\n\t\tZoneA:     p.ZoneAID,\n\t\tZoneB:     p.ZoneBID,\n\t\tP1X:       p.P1X,\n\t\tP1Y:       p.P1Y,\n\t\tP1Z:       p.P1Z,\n\t\tP2X:     p.Width,\n\t\tHeight:    p.Height,\n\t\tEnabled:   p.Enabled,\n\t\tCreatedAt: p.CreatedAt,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ── Zones ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n// listZones returns all zones with current occupancy.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) listZones(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tallZones := h.mgr.GetAllZones()\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tresponse := make([]zoneWithOcc, 0, len(allZones))\n\tfor _, z := range allZones {\n\t\tresponse = append(response, h.toZoneResponse(z))\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n}\n\n// createZone creates a new zone. Auto-generates an ID if none is provided.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) createZone(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar zone zones.Zone\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&zone); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid request body: \"+err.Error())\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif zone.ID == \"\" {\n\t\tzone.ID = \"zone_\" + time.Now().Format(\"20060102-150405\")\n\t}\n\n\tif zone.Name == \"\" {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"name is required\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Set defaults for color\n\tif zone.Color == \"\" {\n\t\tzone.Color = \"#4fc3f7\"\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.mgr.CreateZone(&zone); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to create zone: \"+err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tresp := h.toZoneResponse(h.mgr.GetZone(zone.ID))\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Zone created: %s (%s)\", zone.ID, zone.Name)\n\th.notifyZoneChange(\"created\", h.mgr.GetZone(zone.ID))\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, resp)\n}\n\n// updateZone updates an existing zone.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) updateZone(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n\n\tif h.mgr.GetZone(id) == nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusNotFound, \"zone not found\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tvar zone zones.Zone\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&zone); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid request body: \"+err.Error())\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Preserve the ID from the URL param\n\tzone.ID = id\n\n\tif err := h.mgr.UpdateZone(&zone); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, \"failed to update zone: \"+err.Error())\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tresp := h.toZoneResponse(h.mgr.GetZone(zone.ID))\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Zone updated: %s (%s)\", zone.ID, zone.Name)\n\th.notifyZoneChange(\"updated\", h.mgr.GetZone(zone.ID))\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, resp)\n}\n\n// deleteZone removes a zone by ID.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) deleteZone(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n\n\t// Broadcast before deleting so we can still read the zone.\n\tif z := h.mgr.GetZone(id); z != nil {\n\t\th.notifyZoneChange(\"deleted\", z)\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.mgr.DeleteZone(id); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, \"failed to delete zone: \"+err.Error())\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Zone deleted: %s\", id)\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n}\n\n// historyEntry represents an hourly occupancy bucket for the zone history API.\ntype historyEntry = zones.HistoryEntry\n\n// getZoneHistory returns hourly occupancy history for a zone by querying\n// crossing events from the zones manager's SQLite database.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) getZoneHistory(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n\n\tif h.mgr.GetZone(id) == nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusNotFound, \"zone not found\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tperiod := r.URL.Query().Get(\"period\")\n\tlimit := 24\n\tif period == \"7d\" {\n\t\tlimit = 24 * 7\n\t} else if period == \"30d\" {\n\t\tlimit = 24 * 30\n\t}\n\n\thistory := h.mgr.GetZoneHistory(id, limit)\n\tif history == nil {\n\t\thistory = []historyEntry{}\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, history)\n}\n\n// ── Portals ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n// listPortals returns all portals.\nfunc (h *ZonesHandler) listPortals(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tallPortals := h.mgr.GetAllPortals()\n\n\tresponse := make([]portalWithZones, 0, len(allPortals))\n\tfor _, p := range allPortals {\n\t\tresponse = append(response, toPortalResponse(p))\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n}\n\n// createPortal creates a new portal. {\n550\t\tt.mu.RLock()\n551\t\tdefer t.mu.RUnlock()\n552\t\n553\t\tvar fired []string\n554\t\n555\t\tfor _, trigger := range t.triggers {\n556\t\t\tif !trigger.Enabled {\n557\t\t\t\tcontinue\n558\t\t\t}\n559\t\n560\t\t\t// Check cooldown (5 second minimum refire interval)\n561\t\t\tif trigger.LastFired != nil && time.Since(*trigger.LastFired) < 5*time.Second {\n562\t\t\t\tcontinue\n563\t\t\t}\n564\t\n565\t\t\t// Parse condition params\n566\t\t\tvar params struct {\n567\t\t\t\tDurationS      *int `json:\"duration_s\"`\n568\t\t\t\tCountThreshold *int false\n577\t\t\tswitch trigger.Condition {\n578\t\t\tcase \"enter\", \"leave\":\n579\t\t\t\tif params.VolumeID != \"\" {\n580\t\t\t\t\tfor _, blob := range blobs {\n581\t\t\t\t\t\tif t.engine != nil && t.engine.IsInVolume(blob.X, blob.Y, blob.Z, params.VolumeID) {\n582\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif trigger.Condition == \"enter\" {\n583\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tshouldFire = true\n584\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n585\t\t\t\t\t\t} else \"dwell\":\n593\t\t\t\tif params.DurationS != nil && trigger.LastFired != nil {\n594\t\t\t\t\telapsed := int(time.Since(*trigger.LastFired).Seconds())\n595\t\t\t\t\tif elapsed >= *params.DurationS {\n596\t\t\t\t\t\tshouldFire = true\n597\t\t\t\t\t}\n598\t\t\t\t}\n599\t\t\tcase \"vacant\":\n600\t\t\t\tif len(blobs) == 0 {\n601\t\t\t\t\tshouldFire = true\n602\t\t\t\t}\n603\t\t\tcase \"count\":\n604\t\t\t\tif params.CountThreshold != nil {\n605\t\t\t\t\tif len(blobs) >= *params.CountThreshold {\n606\t\t\t\t\t\tshouldFire = true\n607\t\t\t\t\t}\n608\t\t\t\t}\n609\t\t\t}\n610\t\n611\t\t\tif shouldFire {\n612\t\t\t\tfired = append(fired, trigger.ID)\n613\t\t\t\tnow := time.Now()\n614\t\t\t\ttrigger.LastFired = &now\n615\t\t\t\ttrigger.Elapsed = 0\n616\t\t\t\tt.db.Exec(`UPDATE triggers SET last_fired = ? WHERE id = ?`, now.UnixNano(), trigger.ID)\n617\t\t\t}\n618\t\t}\n619\t\n620\t\treturn fired\n621\t}\n622\t\n623\t// BlobPos represents a blob position for trigger evaluation.\n624\ttype BlobPos *req.Actions\n\t}\n\tt.mu.Unlock()\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, trigger)\n}\n\n// deleteTrigger handles DELETE /api/triggers/{id}.\n//\n// Removes a trigger by ID. *http.Request) {\n\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n\n\tt.mu.RLock()\n\t_, exists := t.triggers[id]\n\tt.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif !exists {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"trigger not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t_, err := t.db.Exec(`DELETE FROM triggers WHERE id = ?`, id)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"failed to delete trigger\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tt.mu.Lock()\n\tdelete(t.triggers, id)\n\tt.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n}\n\n// testTrigger handles POST /api/triggers/{id}/test.\n//\n// Fires the trigger's actions once with a synthetic event payload for testing.\n// If no automation engine is attached, returns a simulated success response.\n// Does not update last_fired or trigger any real automation logic.\n//\n// Response 200 (application/json):\n//\n//\t{\n//\t  \"status\": \"fired\",\n//\t  \"message\": \"Trigger fired successfully\",\n//\t  \"trigger\": { ... }\n//\t}\n//\n// Response 404: trigger not found.\n// Response 500: engine test-fire failed.\nfunc (t *TriggersHandler) testTrigger(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tid := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n\n\tt.mu.RLock()\n\ttrigger, exists := t.triggers[id]\n\tt.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif !exists {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"trigger not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tt.mu.RLock()\n\tengine := t.engine\n\tt.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif engine == nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"status\":  \"ok\",\n\t\t\t\"message\": \"trigger test simulated (no engine attached)\",\n\t\t\t\"trigger\": trigger,\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := engine.TestFire(id); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"test fire failed: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"status\":  \"fired\",\n\t\t\"message\": \"Trigger fired successfully\",\n\t\t\"trigger\": trigger,\n\t})\n}\n\n// ── Trigger evaluation (called by fusion engine) ───────────────────────────────────\n\n// EvaluateTriggers evaluates all enabled triggers against current state.\n// Returns a list of trigger IDs that should fire.\nfunc (t *TriggersHandler) EvaluateTriggers(blobs []BlobPos) []string {\n\tt.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer t.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tvar fired []string\n\n\tfor _, trigger := range t.triggers {\n\t\tif !trigger.Enabled {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check cooldown (5 second minimum refire interval)\n\t\tif trigger.LastFired != nil && time.Since(*trigger.LastFired) < 5*time.Second {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Parse condition params\n\t\tvar params struct {\n\t\t\tDurationS      *int `json:\"duration_s\"`\n\t\t\tCountThreshold *int `json:\"count_threshold\"`\n\t\t\tPersonID       string `json:\"person_id,omitempty\"`\n\t\t\tVolumeID       string `json:\"volume_id,omitempty\"`\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(trigger.ConditionParams) > 0 && string(trigger.ConditionParams) != \"{}\" {\n\t\t\tjson.Unmarshal(trigger.ConditionParams, &params)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tshouldFire := false\n\t\tswitch trigger.Condition {\n\t\tcase \"enter\", \"leave\":\n\t\t\tif params.VolumeID != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, blob := range blobs {\n\t\t\t\t\tif t.engine != nil && t.engine.IsInVolume(blob.X, blob.Y, blob.Z, params.VolumeID) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif trigger.Condition == \"enter\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tshouldFire = true\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif trigger.Condition == \"leave\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tshouldFire = true\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase \"dwell\":\n\t\t\tif params.DurationS != nil && trigger.LastFired != nil {\n\t\t\t\telapsed := int(time.Since(*trigger.LastFired).Seconds())\n\t\t\t\tif elapsed >= *params.DurationS {\n\t\t\t\t\tshouldFire = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase \"vacant\":\n\t\t\tif len(blobs) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tshouldFire = true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase \"count\":\n\t\t\tif params.CountThreshold != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif len(blobs) >= *params.CountThreshold {\n\t\t\t\t\tshouldFire = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif shouldFire {\n\t\t\tfired = append(fired, trigger.ID)\n\t\t\tnow := time.Now()\n\t\t\ttrigger.LastFired = &now\n\t\t\ttrigger.Elapsed = 0\n\t\t\tt.db.Exec(`UPDATE triggers SET last_fired = ? WHERE id = ?`, now.UnixNano(), trigger.ID)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn fired\n}\n\n// BlobPos represents a blob position for trigger evaluation.\ntype BlobPos struct {\n\tID      int\n\tX, Y, Z float64\n}\n","numLines":628,"startLine":1,"totalLines":628}}}

err != nil {\n205\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Reason: \"config must be a JSON object\"}\n206\t\t\t}\n207\t\t}\n208\t\n209\t\tswitch channelType {\n210\t\tcase \"ntfy\":\n211\t\t\t// url is required, token is optional\n212\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; !ok {\n213\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"url\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n214\t\t\t}\n215\t\tcase \"pushover\":\n216\t\t\t// app_token and user_key are required\n217\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"app_token\"]; !ok {\n218\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"app_token\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n219\t\t\t}\n220\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"user_key\"]; !ok {\n221\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"user_key\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n222\t\t\t}\n223\t\tcase \"gotify\":\n224\t\t\t// url and token are required\n225\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; !ok {\n226\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"url\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n227\t\t\t}\n228\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"token\"]; !ok {\n229\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"token\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n230\t\t\t}\n231\t\tcase \"webhook\":\n232\t\t\t// url is required, method and headers are optional\n233\t\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; NotificationsHandler manages notification delivery channels.\n// Supported channel types: ntfy, pushover, gotify, webhook, mqtt.\ntype NotificationsHandler struct {\n\tmu            sync.RWMutex\n\tdb            *sql.DB\n\tchannels      map[string]*NotificationChannel\n\tnotifyService NotifySender\n}\n\n// NotificationChannel represents a notification delivery channel configuration.\n//\n// Channel {\n\t\tenabledInt = 1\n\t}\n\n\tnow := time.Now().UnixMilli()\n\t_, err = n.db.Exec(`\n\t\tINSERT INTO notification_channels (type, enabled, config_json, updated_at)\n\t\tVALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\n\t\tON validates the configuration for a specific channel type.\nfunc validateChannelConfig(channelType string, config interface{}) error {\n\tif config == nil {\n\t\t// Nil config is valid for mqtt, optional for others\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tconfigMap, ok := config.(map[string]interface{})\n\tif !ok {\n\t\t// Try to unmarshal as JSON\n\t\tjsonBytes, err := json.Marshal(config)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Reason: \"config must be a JSON object\"}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(jsonBytes, &configMap); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Reason: \"config must be a JSON object\"}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tswitch channelType {\n\tcase \"ntfy\":\n\t\t// url is required, token is optional\n\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; !ok {\n\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"url\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n\t\t}\n\tcase \"pushover\":\n\t\t// app_token and user_key are required\n\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"app_token\"]; !ok {\n\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"app_token\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"user_key\"]; !ok {\n\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"user_key\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n\t\t}\n\tcase \"gotify\":\n\t\t// url and token are required\n\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; !ok {\n\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"url\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"token\"]; !ok {\n\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"token\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n\t\t}\n\tcase \"webhook\":\n\t\t// url is required, method and headers are optional\n\t\tif _, ok := configMap[\"url\"]; !ok {\n\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"url\", Reason: \"required field missing\"}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Validate method if provided\n\t\tif method, ok := configMap[\"method\"].(string); ok {\n\t\t\tif method != \"GET\" && method != \"POST\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Field: \"method\", Reason: \"must be GET or POST\"}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\tcase \"mqtt\":\n\t\t// No config required for mqtt (uses global connection)\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn &ChannelValidationError{Type: channelType, Reason: \"unknown channel type\"}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ChannelValidationError represents a configuration validation error.\ntype ChannelValidationError struct {\n\tType + \": \" + e.Reason\n}\n\n// RegisterRoutes registers notification endpoints.\n//\n// Notification Channels Endpoints:\n//\n// The notification channels API manages delivery channels for alerts and The channel must be enabled.\n//\t@Tags\t\t\tnotifications\n//\t@Accept\t\t\tjson\n//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n//\t@Param\t\t\trequest\tbody\t\ttestNotificationRequest\ttrue\t\"Test notification parameters\"\n//\t@Success\t\t200\t{object}\tmap[string]interface{}\t\"Test result\"\n//\t@Failure\t\t400\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Invalid request or no enabled channel\"\n//\t@Failure\t\t500\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Failed to send notification\"\n//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/notifications/test [post]\nfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n\tr.Get(\"/api/notifications/config\", n.handleGetConfig)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/notifications/config\", n.handleSetConfig)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/notifications/test\", n.handleSendTest)\n}\n\n// notificationConfigResponse is the response for channel configuration requests.\ntype notificationConfigResponse struct {\n\tChannels map[string]*NotificationChannel `json:\"channels\"`\n}\n\n// handleGetConfig handles GET /api/notifications/config requests.\nfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) handleGetConfig(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, notificationConfigResponse{\n\t\tChannels: n.GetChannels(),\n\t})\n}\n\n// setNotificationConfigRequest is the request body for setting channel configuration.\ntype setNotificationConfigRequest struct {\n\tChannels map[string]struct {\n\t\tType    string      `json:\"type\"`\n\t\tEnabled bool        `json:\"enabled\"`\n\t\tConfig  interface{} `json:\"config,omitempty\"`\n\t} `json:\"channels\"`\n}\n\n// handleSetConfig handles POST /api/notifications/config requests.\nfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) handleSetConfig(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req setNotificationConfigRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid request body: \"+err.Error())\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate and update each channel\n\tfor channelType, ch := range req.Channels {\n\t\tif ch.Type == \"\" {\n\t\t\tch.Type = channelType // Use map key as type if not specified\n\t\t}\n\t\tif ch.Type != channelType {\n\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"channel type mismatch: key is \"+channelType+\" but body specifies \"+ch.Type)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif err := n.SetChannel(ch.Type, ch.Enabled, ch.Config); err != nil {\n\t\t\tif ce, ok := err.(*ChannelValidationError); ok {\n\t\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, ce.Error())\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, \"failed to save configuration: \"+err.Error())\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tn.handleGetConfig(w, r)\n}\n\n// testNotificationRequest is the request body for sending a test notification.\ntype testNotificationRequest struct {\n\tChannelType string                 `json:\"channel_type\"` // ntfy, pushover, gotify, webhook, mqtt\n\tTitle       string                 `json:\"title\"`        // Custom title (optional)\n\tBody        string                 `json:\"body\"`         // Custom body (optional)\n\tData        map[string]interface{} `json:\"data,omitempty\"` // Additional data (optional)\n}\n\n// testNotificationResponse is the response for a test notification.\ntype testNotificationResponse struct {\n\tStatus  string `json:\"status\"`  // \"sent\" or \"simulated\"\n\tMessage string `json:\"message\"` // Human-readable result\n}\n\n// handleSendTest handles POST /api/notifications/test requests.\nfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) handleSendTest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req testNotificationRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid request body: \"+err.Error())\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Set defaults\n\tif req.Title == \"\" {\n\t\treq.Title = \"Spaxel Test Notification\"\n\t}\n\tif req.Body == \"\" {\n\t\treq.Body = \"This is a test notification from Spaxel.\"\n\t}\n\tif req.Data == nil {\n\t\treq.Data = make(map[string]interface{})\n\t}\n\treq.Data[\"test\"] = true\n\n\t// Check if channel type exists and is enabled\n\tch, ok := n.GetChannel(req.ChannelType)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"unknown channel type: \"+req.ChannelType)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif !ch.Enabled {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"channel is not enabled: \"+req.ChannelType)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Send test notification\n\tn.mu.RLock()\n\tsender := n.notifyService\n\tn.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif sender == nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, testNotificationResponse{\n\t\t\tStatus:  \"simulated\",\n\t\t\tMessage: \"Test notification simulated (no sender attached)\",\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := sender.Send(req.Title, req.Body, req.Data); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, \"failed to send notification: \"+err.Error())\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, testNotificationResponse{\n\t\tStatus:  \"sent\",\n\t\tMessage: \"Test notification sent successfully\",\n\t})\n}\n\n// ── Notification sending (called by automation engine) ────────────────────────────\n\n// SendNotification sends a notification via all enabled channels.\nfunc (n *NotificationsHandler) SendNotification(title, body string, data map[string]interface{}) error {\n\tn.mu.RLock()\n\tsender := n.notifyService\n\tchannels := make([]NotificationChannel, 0, len(n.channels))\n\tfor _, ch := range n.channels {\n\t\tif ch.Enabled {\n\t\t\tchannels = append(channels, *ch)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tn.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif len(channels) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tif sender == nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] No notification sender attached, skipping: %s\", title)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn sender.Send(title, body, data)\n}\n","numLines":463,"startLine":1,"totalLines":463}}}

_, s := range h.sessions {\n159\t\t\tsessions = append(sessions, s)\n160\t\t}\n161\t\th.mu.RUnlock()\n162\t\n163\t\t// Get oldest and newest timestamps\n164\t\toldestTS := int64(0)\n165\t\tnewestTS := int64(0)\n166\t\tif stats.HasData {\n167\t\t\t// Scan to find timestamps\n168\t\t\th.scanTimestamps(&oldestTS, &newestTS)\n169\t\t}\n170\t\n171\t\tinfo := replayInfo{\n172\t\t\tHasData:   stats.HasData,\n173\t\t\tFileSize:  stats.FileSize / (1024 * 1024),\n174\t\t\tWritePos:  stats.WritePos,\n175\t\t\tOldestPos: stats.OldestPos,\n176\t\t\tOldestTS:  oldestTS,\n177\t\t\tNewestTS:  newestTS,\n178\t\t\tSessions:  sessions,\n179\t\t}\n180\t\n181\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, info)\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t// scanTimestamps scans the replay store to find oldest and newest timestamps.\n185\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) scanTimestamps(oldest, newest *int64) {\n186\t\tstats := h.worker.GetStoreStats()\n187\t\tif !stats.HasData {\n188\t\t\treturn\n189\t\t}\n190\t\n191\t\t// Scan for oldest and newest\n192\t\tstore := h.worker.GetStore()\n193\t\tstore.Scan(func(recvTimeNS int64, frame []byte) bool {\n194\t\t\trecvMS := recvTimeNS / 1e6\n195\t\t\tif *oldest == 0 || recvMS < *oldest {\n196\t\t\t\t*oldest = recvMS\n197\t\t\t}\n198\t\t\t*newest = recvMS\n199\t\t\treturn true // continue to find newest\n200\t\t})\n201\t}\n202\t\n203\t// startSessionRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/start.\n204\ttype startSessionRequest struct {\n205\t\t// FromISO8601 is the start timestamp in ISO8601 format (e.g., \"2024-03-15T14:30:00Z\")\n206\t\tFromISO8601 string `json:\"from_iso8601\"`\n207\t\t// ToISO8601 is the end timestamp in ISO8601 format. If empty, defaults to now.\n208\t\tToISO8601 string `json:\"to_iso8601\"`\n209\t\t// Speed is the playback speed multiplier: 1, 2, or 5. Defaults to 1.\n210\t\tSpeed int `json:\"speed,omitempty\"`\n211\t}\n212\t\n213\t// startSession handles POST /api/replay/start.\n214\t// Creates a new replay session for the specified time range.\n215\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) startSession(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n216\t\tvar req startSessionRequest\n217\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n218\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n219\t\t\treturn\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\tfromMS, err := parseISO8601(req.FromISO8601)\n223\t\tif err != nil {\n224\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid from_iso8601: \" + err.Error()})\n225\t\t\treturn\n226\t\t}\n227\t\n228\t\ttoMS := timeNowMillis()\n229\t\tif req.ToISO8601 != \"\" {\n230\t\t\ttoMS, err = parseISO8601(req.ToISO8601)\n231\t\t\tif err != nil {\n232\t\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid to_iso8601: \" + err.Error()})\n233\t\t\t\treturn\n234\t\t\t}\n235\t\t}\n236\t\n237\t\tif toMS < fromMS {\n238\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"to_iso8601 must be after from_iso8601\"})\n239\t\t\treturn\n240\t\t}\n241\t\n242\t\tspeed := req.Speed\n243\t\tif speed == 0 {\n244\t\t\tspeed = 1\n245\t\t}\n246\t\tif speed != 1 && speed != 2 && speed != 5 {\n247\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"speed must be 1, 2, or 5\"})\n248\t\t\treturn\n249\t\t}\n250\t\n251\t\t// Start session via worker\n252\t\tsessionID, err := h.worker.StartSession(fromMS, toMS, speed)\n253\t\tif err != nil {\n254\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n255\t\t\treturn\n256\t\t}\n257\t\n258\t\t// Track session in API layer\n259\t\th.mu.Lock()\n260\t\tsession := &_replaySession{\n261\t\t\tID:        sessionID,\n262\t\t\tFromMS:    fromMS,\n263\t\t\tToMS:      toMS,\n264\t\t\tCurrentMS: fromMS,\n265\t\t\tSpeed:     speed,\n266\t\t\tState:     \"paused\",\n267\t\t\tParams:    make(map[string]interface{}),\n268\t\t\tCreatedAt: formatTimestamp(fromMS),\n269\t\t}\n270\t\th.sessions[sessionID] = session\n271\t\th.nextID++\n272\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n273\t\n274\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session started: %s (from %d to %d, speed %dx)\",\n275\t\t\tsessionID, fromMS, toMS, speed)\n276\t\n277\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n278\t\t\t\"session_id\": sessionID,\n279\t\t\t\"from_ms\":    fromMS,\n280\t\t\t\"to_ms\":      toMS,\n281\t\t\t\"speed\":      speed,\n282\t\t\t\"state\":      \"paused\",\n283\t\t})\n284\t}\n285\t\n286\t// stopSessionRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/stop.\n287\ttype stopSessionRequest struct {\n288\t\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to stop.\n289\t\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n290\t}\n291\t\n292\t// stopSession handles POST /api/replay/stop.\n293\t// Stops the specified replay session and deletes it.\n294\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) stopSession(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n295\t\tvar req stopSessionRequest\n296\t\tif err := seekRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/seek.\n323\ttype seekRequest struct {\n324\t\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to seek within.\n325\t\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n326\t\t// TimestampISO8601 is the target timestamp in ISO8601 format.\n327\t\tTimestampISO8601 string `json:\"timestamp_iso8601\"`\n328\t}\n329\t\n330\t// seek handles POST /api/replay/seek.\n331\t// Seeks to the specified timestamp within the session.\n332\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) seek(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n333\t\tvar req seekRequest\n334\t\tif err fromMS || targetMS > toMS {\n359\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"timestamp outside session range\"})\n360\t\t\treturn\n361\t\t}\n362\t\n363\t\tif err := err.Error()})\n369\t\t\treturn\n370\t\t}\n371\t\n372\t\t// Update API layer session state\n373\t\th.mu.Lock()\n374\t\tif s, exists := h.sessions[req.SessionID]; exists {\n375\t\t\ts.CurrentMS = represents the request body for POST /api/replay/tune.\n390\ttype tuneRequest struct {\n391\t\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to tune.\n392\t\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n393\t\t// DeltaRMSThreshold is the motion detection threshold (0.001-1.0).\n394\t\tDeltaRMSThreshold *float64 `json:\"delta_rms_threshold,omitempty\"`\n395\t\t// TauS is the EMA baseline time constant in seconds (1-600).\n396\t\tTauS *float64 `json:\"tau_s,omitempty\"`\n397\t\t// FresnelDecay is the Fresnel zone weight decay rate (1.0-4.0).\n398\t\tFresnelDecay *float64 `json:\"fresnel_decay,omitempty\"`\n399\t\t// Subcarriers is the number of subcarriers for NBVI selection (8-47).\n400\t\tSubcarriers *int `json:\"n_subcarriers,omitempty\"`\n401\t\t// BreathingSensitivity is the breathing detection threshold in radians RMS (0.001-0.1).\n402\t\tBreathingSensitivity *float64 `json:\"breathing_sensitivity,omitempty\"`\n403\t}\n404\t\n405\t// tune /api/replay/set-state.\n511\t// Changes the playback state for a replay session.\n512\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) setState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n513\t\tvar req handles GET /api/replay/session/{id}.\n550\t// Returns the current state of a replay session including blobs.\n551\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) getSessionState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n552\t\tsessionID := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n553\t\n554\t\tsession, err := h.worker.GetSession(sessionID)\n555\t\tif err != nil {\n556\t\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n557\t\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n558\t\t\t\treturn\n559\t\t\t}\n560\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n561\t\t\treturn\n562\t\t}\n563\t\n564\t\t// Update API layer session state\n565\t\th.mu.Lock()\n566\t\tif s, exists := h.sessions[sessionID]; exists {\n567\t\t\ts.CurrentMS = session.CurrentMS\n568\t\t\ts.State = session.State\n569\t\t}\n570\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n571\t\n572\t\t// Calculate progress\n573\t\tduration := session.ToMS - session.FromMS\n574\t\tprogress := 0.0\n575\t\tif duration > 0 {\n576\t\t\tprogress = float64(session.CurrentMS-session.FromMS) / float64(duration)\n577\t\t}\n578\t\n579\t\t// Convert replay blobs to API format\n580\t\tblobs := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(session.LastBlobs))\n581\t\tfor _, b := range session.LastBlobs {\n582\t\t\tblob := map[string]interface{}{\n583\t\t\t\t\"id\":                  b.ID,\n584\t\t\t\t\"x\":                   b.X,\n585\t\t\t\t\"y\":                   b.Y,\n586\t\t\t\t\"z\":                   b.Z,\n587\t\t\t\t\"vx\":                  b.VX,\n588\t\t\t\t\"vy\":                  b.VY,\n589\t\t\t\t\"vz\":                  b.VZ,\n590\t\t\t\t\"weight\":              b.Weight,\n591\t\t\t\t\"posture\":             b.Posture,\n592\t\t\t\t\"person_id\":           b.PersonID,\n593\t\t\t\t\"person_label\":        b.PersonLabel,\n594\t\t\t\t\"person_color\":        b.PersonColor,\n595\t\t\t\t\"identity_confidence\": b.IdentityConfidence,\n596\t\t\t\t\"identity_source\":     b.IdentitySource,\n597\t\t\t\t\"trail\":               b.Trail,\n598\t\t\t}\n599\t\t\tblobs = append(blobs, blob)\n600\t\t}\n601\t\n602\t\t// Build response with session state and blobs\n603\t\tresponse := map[string]interface{}{\n604\t\t\t\"session_id\":  sessionID,\n605\t\t\t\"current_ms\":  session.CurrentMS,\n606\t\t\t\"from_ms\":     session.FromMS,\n607\t\t\t\"to_ms\":       session.ToMS,\n608\t\t\t\"state\":       session.State,\n609\t\t\t\"speed\":       session.Speed,\n610\t\t\t\"progress\":    progress,\n611\t\t\t\"params\":      session.Params,\n612\t\t\t\"blobs\":       blobs,\n613\t\t\t\"timestamp_ms\": session.LastBlobTime,\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t// jumpToTimeRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/jump-to-time.\n620\ttype jumpToTimeRequest struct {\n621\t\t// TimestampMS is the target timestamp in epoch milliseconds.\n622\t\tTimestampMS int64 `json:\"timestamp_ms\"`\n623\t\t// WindowMS is the time window around the timestamp (default 5000ms).\n624\t\tWindowMS int64 `json:\"window_ms,omitempty\"`\n625\t}\n626\t\n627\t// jumpToTime handles POST /api/replay/jump-to-time.\n628\t// Creates a replay session centered on the given timestamp, seeks to it,\n629\t// and sets it as the active session. Stops any previous active session first.\n630\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) jumpToTime(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n631\t\tvar req jumpToTimeRequest\n632\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n633\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n634\t\t\treturn\n635\t\t}\n636\t\n637\t\tif req.TimestampMS <= 0 {\n638\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"timestamp_ms is required and must be positive\"})\n639\t\t\treturn\n640\t\t}\n641\t\n642\t\twindowMS := req.WindowMS\n643\t\tif windowMS <= 0 {\n644\t\t\twindowMS = 5000 // ±5 seconds default\n645\t\t}\n646\t\n647\t\tfromMS := req.TimestampMS - windowMS\n648\t\ttoMS := req.TimestampMS + windowMS\n649\t\n650\t\t// Stop previous active session if any\n651\t\th.mu.Lock()\n652\t\tprevID := h.activeSessionID\n653\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n654\t\n655\t\tif prevID != \"\" {\n656\t\t\tif err := h.worker.StopSession(prevID); err == nil {\n657\t\t\t\th.mu.Lock()\n658\t\t\t\tdelete(h.sessions, prevID)\n659\t\t\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n660\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Previous replay session stopped: %s\", prevID)\n661\t\t\t}\n662\t\t}\n663\t\n664\t\t// Start new session\n665\t\tsessionID, err := the replay store to find oldest and newest timestamps.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) scanTimestamps(oldest, newest *int64) {\n\tstats := h.worker.GetStoreStats()\n\tif !stats.HasData {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Scan for oldest and newest\n\tstore := h.worker.GetStore()\n\tstore.Scan(func(recvTimeNS int64, frame []byte) bool {\n\t\trecvMS := recvTimeNS / 1e6\n\t\tif *oldest == 0 || \"paused\",\n\t\tParams:    make(map[string]interface{}),\n\t\tCreatedAt: formatTimestamp(fromMS),\n\t}\n\th.sessions[sessionID] = session\n\th.nextID++\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] stopSessionRequest struct {\n\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to stop.\n\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n}\n\n// stopSession handles POST /api/replay/stop.\n// Stops the specified replay session and deletes it.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) stopSession(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req stopSessionRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.worker.StopSession(req.SessionID); err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdelete(h.sessions, req.SessionID)\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session stopped: %s\", req.SessionID)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"status\":  \"stopped\",\n\t\t\"session\": req.SessionID,\n\t})\n}\n\n// seekRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/seek.\ntype seekRequest struct {\n\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to seek within.\n\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n\t// TimestampISO8601 is the target timestamp in ISO8601 format.\n\tTimestampISO8601 string `json:\"timestamp_iso8601\"`\n}\n\n// seek handles POST /api/replay/seek.\n// Seeks to the specified timestamp within the session.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) seek(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req seekRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\ttargetMS, err := parseISO8601(req.TimestampISO8601)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid timestamp: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tsession, err := h.worker.GetSession(req.SessionID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tfromMS := session.FromMS\n\ttoMS := session.ToMS\n\n\tif targetMS < fromMS || targetMS > toMS {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"timestamp outside session range\"})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.worker.Seek(req.SessionID, targetMS); err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"timestamp outside session range\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"timestamp outside session range\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update API layer session state\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tif s, exists := h.sessions[req.SessionID]; exists {\n\t\ts.CurrentMS = targetMS\n\t\ts.State = \"paused\"\n\t}\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session seeked: %s to %d\", req.SessionID, targetMS)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"status\":      \"seeked\",\n\t\t\"current_ms\":  targetMS,\n\t\t\"frame_found\": true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// tuneRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/tune.\ntype tuneRequest struct {\n\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to tune.\n\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n\t// DeltaRMSThreshold is the motion detection threshold (0.001-1.0).\n\tDeltaRMSThreshold *float64 `json:\"delta_rms_threshold,omitempty\"`\n\t// TauS is the EMA baseline time constant in seconds (1-600).\n\tTauS *float64 `json:\"tau_s,omitempty\"`\n\t// FresnelDecay is the Fresnel zone weight decay rate (1.0-4.0).\n\tFresnelDecay *float64 `json:\"fresnel_decay,omitempty\"`\n\t// Subcarriers is the number of subcarriers for NBVI selection (8-47).\n\tSubcarriers *int `json:\"n_subcarriers,omitempty\"`\n\t// BreathingSensitivity is the breathing detection threshold in radians RMS (0.001-0.1).\n\tBreathingSensitivity *float64 `json:\"breathing_sensitivity,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// tune handles POST /api/replay/tune.\n// Updates pipeline parameters for the replay session.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) tune(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req tuneRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif _, err := h.worker.GetSession(req.SessionID); err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build params map\n\tparams := make(map[string]interface{})\n\tif req.DeltaRMSThreshold != nil {\n\t\tparams[\"delta_rms_threshold\"] = *req.DeltaRMSThreshold\n\t}\n\tif req.TauS != nil {\n\t\tparams[\"tau_s\"] = *req.TauS\n\t}\n\tif req.FresnelDecay != nil {\n\t\tparams[\"fresnel_decay\"] = *req.FresnelDecay\n\t}\n\tif req.Subcarriers != nil {\n\t\tparams[\"n_subcarriers\"] = *req.Subcarriers\n\t}\n\tif req.BreathingSensitivity != nil {\n\t\tparams[\"breathing_sensitivity\"] = *req.BreathingSensitivity\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.worker.UpdateParams(req.SessionID, params); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session tuned: %s params=%+v\", req.SessionID, params)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"status\":  \"tuned\",\n\t\t\"params\":  params,\n\t\t\"session\": req.SessionID,\n\t})\n}\n\n// setSpeedRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/set-speed.\ntype setSpeedRequest struct {\n\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to modify.\n\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n\t// Speed is the playback speed multiplier: 1, 2, or 5.\n\tSpeed int `json:\"speed\"`\n}\n\n// setSpeed handles POST /api/replay/set-speed.\n// Changes the playback speed for a replay session.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) setSpeed(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req setSpeedRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif req.Speed != 1 && req.Speed != 2 && req.Speed != 5 {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"speed must be 1, 2, or 5\"})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.worker.SetPlaybackSpeed(req.SessionID, req.Speed); err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update API layer session state\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tif s, exists := h.sessions[req.SessionID]; exists {\n\t\ts.Speed = req.Speed\n\t}\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session speed changed: %s to %dx\", req.SessionID, req.Speed)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"status\":  \"speed_set\",\n\t\t\"session\": req.SessionID,\n\t\t\"speed\":   req.Speed,\n\t})\n}\n\n// setStateRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/set-state.\ntype setStateRequest struct {\n\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to modify.\n\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n\t// State is the playback state: \"playing\" or \"paused\".\n\tState string `json:\"state\"`\n}\n\n// setState handles POST /api/replay/set-state.\n// Changes the playback state for a replay session.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) setState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req setStateRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif req.State != \"playing\" && req.State != \"paused\" {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"state must be 'playing' or 'paused'\"})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.worker.SetState(req.SessionID, req.State); err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update API layer session state\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tif s, exists := h.sessions[req.SessionID]; exists {\n\t\ts.State = req.State\n\t}\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session state changed: %s to %s\", req.SessionID, req.State)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"status\":  \"state_set\",\n\t\t\"session\": req.SessionID,\n\t\t\"state\":   req.State,\n\t})\n}\n\n// getSessionState handles GET /api/replay/session/{id}.\n// Returns the current state of a replay session including blobs.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) getSessionState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tsessionID := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n\n\tsession, err := h.worker.GetSession(sessionID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update API layer session state\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tif s, exists := h.sessions[sessionID]; exists {\n\t\ts.CurrentMS = session.CurrentMS\n\t\ts.State = session.State\n\t}\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Calculate progress\n\tduration := session.ToMS - session.FromMS\n\tprogress := 0.0\n\tif duration > 0 {\n\t\tprogress = float64(session.CurrentMS-session.FromMS) / float64(duration)\n\t}\n\n\t// Convert replay blobs to API format\n\tblobs := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(session.LastBlobs))\n\tfor _, b := range session.LastBlobs {\n\t\tblob := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"id\":                  b.ID,\n\t\t\t\"x\":                   b.X,\n\t\t\t\"y\":                   b.Y,\n\t\t\t\"z\":                   b.Z,\n\t\t\t\"vx\":                  b.VX,\n\t\t\t\"vy\":                  b.VY,\n\t\t\t\"vz\":                  b.VZ,\n\t\t\t\"weight\":              b.Weight,\n\t\t\t\"posture\":             b.Posture,\n\t\t\t\"person_id\":           b.PersonID,\n\t\t\t\"person_label\":        b.PersonLabel,\n\t\t\t\"person_color\":        b.PersonColor,\n\t\t\t\"identity_confidence\": b.IdentityConfidence,\n\t\t\t\"identity_source\":     b.IdentitySource,\n\t\t\t\"trail\":               b.Trail,\n\t\t}\n\t\tblobs = append(blobs, blob)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build response with session state and blobs\n\tresponse := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"session_id\":  sessionID,\n\t\t\"current_ms\":  session.CurrentMS,\n\t\t\"from_ms\":     session.FromMS,\n\t\t\"to_ms\":       session.ToMS,\n\t\t\"state\":       session.State,\n\t\t\"speed\":       session.Speed,\n\t\t\"progress\":    progress,\n\t\t\"params\":      session.Params,\n\t\t\"blobs\":       blobs,\n\t\t\"timestamp_ms\": session.LastBlobTime,\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n}\n\n// jumpToTimeRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/jump-to-time.\ntype jumpToTimeRequest struct {\n\t// TimestampMS is the target timestamp in epoch milliseconds.\n\tTimestampMS int64 `json:\"timestamp_ms\"`\n\t// WindowMS is the time window around the timestamp (default 5000ms).\n\tWindowMS int64 `json:\"window_ms,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// jumpToTime handles POST /api/replay/jump-to-time.\n// Creates a replay session centered on the given timestamp, seeks to it,\n// and sets it as the active session. Stops any previous active session first.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) jumpToTime(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req jumpToTimeRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif req.TimestampMS <= 0 {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"timestamp_ms is required and must be positive\"})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\twindowMS := req.WindowMS\n\tif windowMS <= 0 {\n\t\twindowMS = 5000 // ±5 seconds default\n\t}\n\n\tfromMS := req.TimestampMS - windowMS\n\ttoMS := req.TimestampMS + windowMS\n\n\t// Stop previous active session if any\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tprevID := h.activeSessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif prevID != \"\" {\n\t\tif err := h.worker.StopSession(prevID); err == nil {\n\t\t\th.mu.Lock()\n\t\t\tdelete(h.sessions, prevID)\n\t\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Previous replay session stopped: %s\", prevID)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Start new session\n\tsessionID, err := h.worker.StartSession(fromMS, toMS, 1)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": \"failed to start session: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Seek to target timestamp\n\tif err := h.worker.Seek(sessionID, req.TimestampMS); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Jump-to-time seek failed: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Track in API layer\n\tsession := &_replaySession{\n\t\tID:        sessionID,\n\t\tFromMS:    fromMS,\n\t\tToMS:      toMS,\n\t\tCurrentMS: req.TimestampMS,\n\t\tSpeed:     1,\n\t\tState:     \"paused\",\n\t\tParams:    make(map[string]interface{}),\n\t\tCreatedAt: formatTimestamp(fromMS),\n\t}\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\th.sessions[sessionID] = session\n\th.activeSessionID = sessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Jump-to-time: session %s at %d (window ±%dms)\", sessionID, req.TimestampMS, windowMS)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"session_id\":   sessionID,\n\t\t\"timestamp_ms\": req.TimestampMS,\n\t\t\"from_ms\":      fromMS,\n\t\t\"to_ms\":        toMS,\n\t\t\"state\":        \"paused\",\n\t})\n}\n\n// Helper functions\nfunc timeNowMillis() int64 {\n\treturn time.Now().UnixNano() / 1e6\n}\n\nfunc parseISO8601(s string) (int64, error) {\n\tt, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, err\n\t}\n\treturn t.UnixNano() / 1e6, nil\n}\n\nfunc formatTimestamp(ms int64) string {\n\treturn time.Unix(ms/1000, (ms%1000)*1e6).Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n}\n\n// GetReplayPath returns the path to the CSI replay binary file.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) GetReplayPath() string {\n\tif h.replayPath != \"\" {\n\t\treturn h.replayPath\n\t}\n\treturn \"/data/csi_replay.bin\" // Default path\n}\n\n// SetReplayPath sets the path to the CSI replay binary file.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) SetReplayPath(path string) {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n\th.replayPath = path\n}\n\n// GetStoreStats returns statistics about the replay store.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) GetStoreStats() replay.StoreStats {\n\treturn h.worker.GetStoreStats()\n}\n\n// GetSessions returns a list of all active replay sessions.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) GetSessions() []SessionInfo {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tsessions := make([]SessionInfo, 0, len(h.sessions))\n\tfor _, s := range h.sessions {\n\t\tsessions = append(sessions, SessionInfo{\n\t\t\tID:        s.ID,\n\t\t\tFromMS:    s.FromMS,\n\t\t\tToMS:      s.ToMS,\n\t\t\tCurrentMS: s.CurrentMS,\n\t\t\tState:     s.State,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\treturn sessions\n}\n\n// SeekTo moves the active replay session to the target timestamp.\n// Implements dashboard.ReplayHandler interface.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) SeekTo(targetMS int64) error {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tsessionID := h.activeSessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif sessionID == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no active replay session\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn h.worker.Seek(sessionID, targetMS)\n}\n\n// Play starts playback of the active replay session at the specified speed.\n// Implements dashboard.ReplayHandler interface.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) Play(speed float64) error {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tsessionID := h.activeSessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif sessionID == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no active replay session\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Convert float speed to int (1x=1, 2x=2, 0.5x=1 for now)\n\tspeedInt := 1\n\tif speed >= 2.0 {\n\t\tspeedInt = 2\n\t} else if speed >= 5.0 {\n\t\tspeedInt = 5\n\t}\n\n\t// Set speed first\n\tif err := h.worker.SetPlaybackSpeed(sessionID, speedInt); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Then set state to playing\n\treturn h.worker.SetState(sessionID, \"playing\")\n}\n\n// Pause pauses playback of the active replay session.\n// Implements dashboard.ReplayHandler interface.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) Pause() error {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tsessionID := h.activeSessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif sessionID == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no active replay session\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn h.worker.SetState(sessionID, \"paused\")\n}\n\n// SetParams updates the replay pipeline parameters for the active session.\n// Implements dashboard.ReplayHandler interface.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) SetParams(params *replay.TunableParams) error {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tsessionID := h.activeSessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif sessionID == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no active replay session\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Convert TunableParams to map for worker\n\tparamMap := make(map[string]interface{})\n\tif params.DeltaRMSThreshold != nil {\n\t\tparamMap[\"delta_rms_threshold\"] = *params.DeltaRMSThreshold\n\t}\n\tif params.TauS != nil {\n\t\tparamMap[\"tau_s\"] = *params.TauS\n\t}\n\tif params.FresnelDecay != nil {\n\t\tparamMap[\"fresnel_decay\"] = *params.FresnelDecay\n\t}\n\tif params.FresnelWeightSigma != nil {\n\t\tparamMap[\"fresnel_weight_sigma\"] = *params.FresnelWeightSigma\n\t}\n\tif params.MinConfidence != nil {\n\t\tparamMap[\"min_confidence\"] = *params.MinConfidence\n\t}\n\tif params.BreathingSensitivity != nil {\n\t\tparamMap[\"breathing_sensitivity\"] = *params.BreathingSensitivity\n\t}\n\tif params.NSubcarriers != nil {\n\t\tparamMap[\"n_subcarriers\"] = *params.NSubcarriers\n\t}\n\n\treturn h.worker.UpdateParams(sessionID, paramMap)\n}\n\n// ApplyToLive copies the current replay parameters to the live configuration.\n// Implements dashboard.ReplayHandler interface.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) ApplyToLive() error {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tsessionID := h.activeSessionID\n\tsettingsHandler := h.settingsHandler\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif sessionID == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no active replay session\")\n\t}\n\n\tif settingsHandler == nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] ApplyToLive: No settings handler configured, parameters not persisted\")\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"settings handler not configured\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the current session's parameters\n\tsession, err := h.worker.GetSession(sessionID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Convert replay params to settings format\n\tupdates := make(map[string]interface{})\n\n\t// Map replay parameters to live settings\n\tif val, ok := session.Params[\"delta_rms_threshold\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := val.(float64); ok {\n\t\t\tupdates[\"delta_rms_threshold\"] = f\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif val, ok := session.Params[\"tau_s\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := val.(float64); ok {\n\t\t\tupdates[\"tau_s\"] = f\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif val, ok := session.Params[\"fresnel_decay\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := val.(float64); ok {\n\t\t\tupdates[\"fresnel_decay\"] = f\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif val, ok := session.Params[\"fresnel_weight_sigma\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := val.(float64); ok {\n\t\t\tupdates[\"fresnel_weight_sigma\"] = f\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif val, ok := session.Params[\"min_confidence\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := val.(float64); ok {\n\t\t\tupdates[\"min_confidence\"] = f\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif val, ok := session.Params[\"breathing_sensitivity\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := val.(float64); ok {\n\t\t\tupdates[\"breathing_sensitivity\"] = f\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif val, ok := session.Params[\"n_subcarriers\"]; ok {\n\t\tif i, ok := val.(int); ok {\n\t\t\tupdates[\"n_subcarriers\"] = i\n\t\t} else if f, ok := val.(float64); ok {\n\t\t\tupdates[\"n_subcarriers\"] = int(f)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(updates) == 0 {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] ApplyToLive: No replay parameters to apply\")\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Persist to settings database\n\tif err := settingsHandler.Update(updates); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] ApplyToLive: Failed to persist parameters: %v\", err)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to persist parameters: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] ApplyToLive: Applied replay parameters to live: %+v\", updates)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// SetSpeed changes the playback speed of the active replay session.\n// Implements dashboard.ReplayHandler interface.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) SetSpeed(speed float64) error {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tsessionID := h.activeSessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif sessionID == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no active replay session\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Convert float speed to int\n\tspeedInt := 1\n\tif speed >= 2.0 {\n\t\tspeedInt = 2\n\t} else if speed >= 5.0 {\n\t\tspeedInt = {\n199\t\t// Device endpoints\n200\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices\", h.listDevices)\n201\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.getDevice)\n202\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}/history\", h.getDeviceHistory)\n203\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}/aliases\", h.getDeviceAliases)\n204\t\tr.Put(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.updateDevice)\n205\t\tr.Delete(\"/api/ble/devices/{mac}\", h.archiveDevice)\n206\t\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/devices/preregister\", h.preregisterDevice)\n207\t\n208\t\t// Duplicate detection\n209\t\tr.Get(\"/api/ble/duplicates\", h.listDuplicates)\n210\t\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/merge\", h.mergeDevices)\n211\t\tr.Post(\"/api/ble/split\", h.splitDevice)\n212\t\n213\t\t// People endpoints\n214\t\tr.Get(\"/api/people\", h.listPeople)\n215\t\tr.Post(\"/api/people\", h.createPerson)\n216\t\tr.Get(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.getPerson)\n217\t\tr.Put(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.updatePerson)\n218\t\tr.Delete(\"/api/people/{id}\", h.deletePerson)\n219\t}\n220\t\n221\t// ── Device endpoints ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n222\t\n223\t// listDevices handles GET /api/ble/devices.\n224\t//\n225\t// Returns a list of all BLE devices seen by the system. Devices can be filtered\n226\t// by registration status (registered/discovered), time window (hours parameter),\n227\t// and archival status.\n228\t//\n229\t// Query parameters:\n230\t//   - registered: \"true\" to return only devices assigned to a person\n231\t//   - discovered: \"true\" to return only unassigned devices\n232\t//   - archived: \"true\" to include archived (soft-deleted) devices\n233\t//   - hours: time window in hours (default: 24)\n234\t//\n235\t// Response: JSON object with \"devices\" array and \"privacy_notice\" string.\n236\t// Each device includes: mac, name, label, manufacturer, device_type, device_name,\n237\t// person_id, person_name, rssi_min, rssi_max, rssi_avg, first_seen_at, last_seen_at,\n238\t// last_seen_node, is_archived, is_wearable, enabled, last_location.\n239\t//\n240\t// Status codes:\n241\t//   - 200: Success\n242\t//   - 500: Internal error\n243\tfunc (h *Handler) listDevices(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n244\t\tincludeArchived := r.URL.Query().Get(\"archived\") == \"true\"\n245\t\tregistered := r.URL.Query().Get(\"registered\")\n246\t\tdiscovered := r.URL.Query().Get(\"discovered\")\n247\t\n248\t\t// Parse hours parameter (default: 24 hours)\n249\t\thoursStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"hours\")\n250\t\thours := 24 // Default to last 24 hours\n251\t\tif hoursStr != \"\" {\n252\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(hoursStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n253\t\t\t\thours = n\n254\t\t\t}\n255\t\t}\n256\t\n257\t\tvar devices []DeviceRecord\n258\t\tvar err error\n259\t\n260\t\t// Filter by registration status and time window\n261\t\tif registered == \"true\" {\n262\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n263\t\t\t// Filter to only registered devices (has person_id)\n264\t\t\tvar registeredDevices []DeviceRecord\n265\t\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n266\t\t\t\tif d.PersonID != \"\" {\n267\t\t\t\t\tregisteredDevices = append(registeredDevices, d)\n268\t\t\t\t}\n269\t\t\t}\n270\t\t\tdevices = registeredDevices\n271\t\t} else if discovered == \"true\" {\n272\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n273\t\t\t// Filter to only unregistered devices (no person_id)\n274\t\t\tvar unregisteredDevices []DeviceRecord\n275\t\t\tfor _, d := range devices {\n276\t\t\t\tif d.PersonID == \"\" {\n277\t\t\t\t\tunregisteredDevices = append(unregisteredDevices, d)\n278\t\t\t\t}\n279\t\t\t}\n280\t\t\tdevices = unregisteredDevices\n281\t\t} else {\n282\t\t\t// Get all devices seen in the time window\n283\t\t\tdevices, err = h.registry.GetDevicesSeenInHours(hours, includeArchived)\n284\t\t}\n285\t\n286\t\tif err != nil {\n287\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n288\t\t\treturn\n289\t\t}\n290\t\tif devices == nil {\n291\t\t\tdevices = []DeviceRecord{}\n292\t\t}\n293\t\n294\t\t// Add privacy notice in response header\n295\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Privacy-Notice\", \"Phones may appear multiple times due to address rotation. Wearables and AirTags have stable addresses.\")\n296\t\n297\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n298\t\t\t\"devices\":       devices,\n299\t\t\t\"privacy_notice\": \"Phones may appear multiple times due to address rotation. Wearables and AirTags have stable addresses.\",\n300\t\t})\n301\t}\n302\t\n303\t// getDevice handles GET /api/ble/devices/{mac}.\n304\t//\n305\t// Returns detailed information about a single BLE device by its MAC address.\n306\t// The MAC address should be in uppercase colon-separated hex format (e.g., \"AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF\").\n307\t//\n308\t// URL parameters:\n309\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address\n310\t//\n311\t// Response: JSON device object with all fields including location history.\n312\t//\n313\t// Status codes:\n314\t//   - 200: Success, device found\n315\t//   - 404: Device not found\n316\t//   - 500: Internal error\n317\tfunc (h *Handler) getDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n318\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n319\t\tdevice, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac)\n320\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n321\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n322\t\t\treturn\n323\t\t}\n324\t\tif err != nil {\n325\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n326\t\t\treturn\n327\t\t}\n328\t\twriteJSON(w, device)\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// getDeviceHistory handles GET /api/ble/devices/{mac}/history.\n332\t//\n333\t// Returns the sighting history for a specific BLE device. This includes\n334\t// RSSI observations from nodes that have detected this device over time.\n335\t//\n336\t// URL parameters:\n337\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address\n338\t//\n339\t// Query parameters:\n340\t//   - limit: maximum number of history entries to return (default: 100, max: 1000)\n341\t//\n342\t// Response: JSON object with \"mac\", \"history\" (array of sighting entries),\n343\t// and \"limit\" fields. Each history entry includes timestamp, rssi_dbm, and node_mac.\n344\t//\n345\t// Status codes:\n346\t//   - 200: Success\n347\t//   - 404: Device not found\n348\t//   - 500: Internal error\n349\tfunc (h *Handler) getDeviceHistory(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n350\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n351\t\n352\t\t// Parse limit parameter\n353\t\tlimitStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"limit\")\n354\t\tlimit := 100\n355\t\tif limitStr != \"\" {\n356\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(limitStr); err == nil && n > 0 {\n357\t\t\t\tlimit = n\n358\t\t\t}\n359\t\t}\n360\t\n361\t\t// Check that the device exists first\n362\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); err != nil {\n363\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {\n364\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"device not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n365\t\t\t\treturn\n366\t\t\t}\n367\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n368\t\t\treturn\n369\t\t}\n370\t\n371\t\thistory, err := h.registry.GetDeviceSightingHistory(mac, limit)\n372\t\tif err != nil {\n373\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"internal error\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n374\t\t\treturn\n375\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\twriteJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{\n378\t\t\t\"mac\":     mac,\n379\t\t\t\"history\": history,\n380\t\t\t\"limit\":   limit,\n381\t\t})\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\ttype updateDeviceRequest struct {\n385\t\tLabel      string `json:\"label\"`       // User-assigned display label\n386\t\tDeviceType string `json:\"device_type\"` // Device type (apple_phone, apple_watch, tile, etc.)\n387\t\tPersonID   string `json:\"person_id\"`   // Person ID to assign device to\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// updateDevice handles PUT /api/ble/devices/{mac}.\n391\t//\n392\t// Updates a BLE device's properties. This endpoint is used to set a human-readable\n393\t// label for a device and/or assign it to a person for identity tracking.\n394\t//\n395\t// URL parameters:\n396\t//   - mac: BLE device MAC address (uppercase colon-separated hex)\n397\t//\n398\t// Request body: JSON object with optional fields:\n399\t//   - label: User-assigned display label (e.g., \"Alice's iPhone\")\n400\t//   - device_type: Device type identifier (e.g., \"apple_phone\", \"tile\", \"fitbit\")\n401\t//   - person_id: UUID of person to assign this device to (must exist)\n402\t//\n403\t// Response: Updated device object as JSON.\n404\t//\n405\t// Status codes:\n406\t//   - 200: Success, device updated\n407\t//   - 400: Invalid request body or person_id not found\n408\t//   - 404: Device not found\n409\t//   - 500: Internal error\n410\tfunc (h *Handler) updateDevice(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n411\t\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n412\t\n413\t\t// Verify device exists\n414\t\tif _, err := h.registry.GetDevice(mac); http.StatusInternalServerError)\n796\t\t\treturn\n797\t\t}\n798\t\n799\t\t// Get the split device\n800\t\tsplitDevice, _ := h.registry.GetDevice(req.AliasAddr)\n801\t\n802\t\twriteJSON(w,
